segments.text:
L101 – Lab 12
Term project
Draft of journal article summary due today.
Final Term project paper (Pg. 5-13 – 5-15) due Dec. 8
Antibiotic resistance experiment
Measure zone of inhibition
Review of experimental design
Analyze data and create graphs
Complete questions; work in groups of 2
Discussion of Kappell et al. (2015)

Summary for one primary research article
Describe the system.
Explain the question(s), hypothesis and specific prediction of the experiment that the authors were testing
Explain how question was tested (methods/background)
Describe results (include figure or table).  Do these results support the hypothesis?
How do these results relate to the questions asked or link to another study that you will discuss in your paper (conclusion)
Must write in your own words ( no quotes , no plagiarism)
Must cite paper in text and provide complete citation list at end
Must include figure or table (can rework figure or table) at end of summary
Length ~2 pages double-spaced (does not include figures or tables)
Examples in Canvas (Lab 11 and 12 Module)

Summary of Our E. coli Project
Testing two strains of E. coli
Lab strain (W) – no exposure to antibiotics
St. Joseph River strains (SJ) – unknown exposure to antibiotics
Questions:
Do the SJ and W strains of E. coli differ in their initial levels of antibiotic resistance?
Can E. coli exposed to concentrations of tetracycline evolve resistance to tetracycline?
Can resistance to other antibiotics increase even if the E. coli are not directly exposed to those other antibiotics?

Day 1 (Lab 11) Transfer #1; Collect ZOI data
Day 2 (Transfer #2)
Day 3 (Transfer #3)
Day 4 (Transfer #4)
Day 5: Set up final ZOI test
Day 6: Measure final ZOI in lab 12

Data Analysis, Presenting data, and Interpreting data
Calculate summary statistics - mean  STDEV; % change
Decide how to graph these results
Describe and interpret the results
Looking at the bigger picture

Density of trees in 3 forests.  Tree density measured for 10 plots per forest.
What is the mean tree density for each forest?
Which forest shows greatest variation in tree density per plot?
Number of trees per 100 m 2
Forest 1
Forest 2
Forest 3

Y-axis label = % decrease in zone of inhibition
If there is a decrease in the zone of inhibition between
initial and final measurements, does this indicate an
increase or decrease in antibiotic resistance?

The role of natural selection in evolving drug resistance
fewer
drugs
more drugs

Hypothesis: E. coli isolated from urban waterways will have similar or higher amounts of antibiotic resistance compared to human sources of E. coli
E. Coli tested from clinics (n = 99), human derived sewage (n = 66), inner harbor (n = 36) and outer harbor (n = 58)

Determining level of resistance
Tested E. coli resistance to 17 antibiotics
Well with
no antibiotic
Well with
8 ug /mL of antibiotic
Well with
16 ug /mL of antibiotic
Well with
32 ug /mL of antibiotic

Kappell et al. (2015)
1) Background questions from the Introduction
How do the authors define multi-drug resistance (MDR)?
To which antibiotics is E. coli often resistant?
2) Review experimental design for Kappell et al. study
What were the independent and dependent (measurement) variables in the study?
What were the levels of replication in this experiment?
What were the criteria for determining when a particular E. coli isolate was resistant to an antibiotic?

Figure 1 ( Kappell et al. 2015)
90%+ of the strains collected in the outer Milwaukee river harbor showed resistance to ampicillin and tetracycline.
Did we see resistance to these antibiotics in the St. Joseph river strains that we tested?

Figure 2 ( Kappell et al. 2015)
Figure 2. Percentage of E. coli isolates from sewage (n=66), outer harbor (n=58), inner harbor (n=36), and clinical setting (n=99) showing number of antibiotic resistant isolates (resistant level).
